prompt: Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_release` that reads an EscrowDatum (with fields beneficiary: ByteArray and amount: Int) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify InlineDatum(raw) pattern, deserialize to EscrowDatum, and check that the beneficiary signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_validator` with a SpendDatum (min_price: Int, owner: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains an OracleDatum with price >= min_price and that the owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `multisig_wallet` with a MultiSigDatum (required_signers: List<ByteArray>, threshold: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least threshold of the required_signers appear in extra_signatories.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `loan_repay` with LoanDatum (borrower: ByteArray, principal: Int, interest: Int, due_date: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow repayment if borrower signed and amount >= principal + interest.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `treasury_spend` with ProposalDatum (recipient: ByteArray, amount: Int, votes_for: Int, votes_against: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend if votes_for > votes_against.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `collateral_vault` with CollateralDatum (borrower: ByteArray, loan_ref: ByteArray, liquidation_ratio: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow liquidation only if borrower signed or loan is in default.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `safe_escrow` with EscrowDatum via InlineDatum. Handle all three datum constructors: InlineDatum (proceed), DatumHash (reject with fail), NoDatum (reject with fail). Only process when datum is inline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `bridge_release` with BridgeDatum (amount: Int, recipient: ByteArray, nonce: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Verify the nonce hasn't been used by checking a reference input's NonceDatum.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `acl_gate` with AccessDatum (allowed: List<ByteArray>, admin: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow transaction if any signer is in the allowed list or is the admin.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `upgradeable` with VersionedDatum (version: Int, admin: ByteArray, params: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow upgrade (version bump) only by admin; allow use by anyone when active.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `dao_execute` with ProposalDatum (proposal_id: ByteArray, executor: ByteArray, quorum_met: Bool) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if quorum_met is True and executor signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `insurance_claim` with PolicyDatum (insured: ByteArray, coverage: Int, event_id: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow claim if insured signed and event matches datum event_id.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_release` that reads an EscrowDatum (with fields beneficiary: ByteArray and amount: Int) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify InlineDatum(raw) pattern, deserialize to EscrowDatum, and check that the beneficiary signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `oracle_consumer` that reads an OracleDatum (price: Int, timestamp: Int, source: ByteArray) from a reference input using InlineDatum. Verify the price is above a minimum threshold stored in the spend datum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_validator` with a SpendDatum (min_price: Int, owner: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains an OracleDatum with price >= min_price and that the owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_bid` with AuctionDatum (highest_bid: Int, bidder: ByteArray, deadline: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds current highest_bid and deadline hasn't passed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_close` that reads AuctionDatum (winner: ByteArray, amount: Int, seller: ByteArray) from InlineDatum. Allow close only after deadline and verify winner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `multisig_wallet` with a MultiSigDatum (required_signers: List<ByteArray>, threshold: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least threshold of the required_signers appear in extra_signatories.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `protocol_config` with ConfigDatum (admin: ByteArray, fee_bps: Int, paused: Bool) using InlineDatum. Allow actions only when not paused and admin signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `lp_withdraw` with PoolDatum (reserve_a: Int, reserve_b: Int, lp_supply: Int) using InlineDatum. Verify the LP token burn amount matches the proportional share of reserves.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `treasury_spend` with ProposalDatum (recipient: ByteArray, amount: Int, votes_for: Int, votes_against: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend if votes_for > votes_against.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `subscription` with SubDatum (subscriber: ByteArray, expiry: Int, fee_per_period: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow renewal if subscriber signed and fee is paid; allow cancel after expiry.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `collateral_vault` with CollateralDatum (borrower: ByteArray, loan_ref: ByteArray, liquidation_ratio: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow liquidation only if borrower signed or loan is in default.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `bridge_release` with BridgeDatum (amount: Int, recipient: ByteArray, nonce: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Verify the nonce hasn't been used by checking a reference input's NonceDatum.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `acl_gate` with AccessDatum (allowed: List<ByteArray>, admin: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow transaction if any signer is in the allowed list or is the admin.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `upgradeable` with VersionedDatum (version: Int, admin: ByteArray, params: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow upgrade (version bump) only by admin; allow use by anyone when active.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `dao_execute` with ProposalDatum (proposal_id: ByteArray, executor: ByteArray, quorum_met: Bool) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if quorum_met is True and executor signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `insurance_claim` with PolicyDatum (insured: ByteArray, coverage: Int, event_id: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow claim if insured signed and event matches datum event_id.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_release` that reads an EscrowDatum (with fields beneficiary: ByteArray and amount: Int) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify InlineDatum(raw) pattern, deserialize to EscrowDatum, and check that the beneficiary signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_timeout` with EscrowDatum containing beneficiary: ByteArray, deadline: Int, and refund_to: ByteArray. Use InlineDatum to read the datum from the continuing output. Allow release if beneficiary signed and deadline not passed, or refund if deadline passed and owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `oracle_consumer` that reads an OracleDatum (price: Int, timestamp: Int, source: ByteArray) from a reference input using InlineDatum. Verify the price is above a minimum threshold stored in the spend datum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_validator` with a SpendDatum (min_price: Int, owner: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains an OracleDatum with price >= min_price and that the owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_cliff` with VestingDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, cliff_time: Int, total_amount: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal only after cliff_time using interval.is_entirely_after.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_linear` with VestingDatum (owner: ByteArray, start_time: Int, end_time: Int, amount: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the owner signed and the transaction validity range is within vesting period.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_bid` with AuctionDatum (highest_bid: Int, bidder: ByteArray, deadline: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds current highest_bid and deadline hasn't passed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_close` that reads AuctionDatum (winner: ByteArray, amount: Int, seller: ByteArray) from InlineDatum. Allow close only after deadline and verify winner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `multisig_wallet` with a MultiSigDatum (required_signers: List<ByteArray>, threshold: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least threshold of the required_signers appear in extra_signatories.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nested_config` where the datum contains a nested type: pub type Limits { min: Int, max: Int } inside pub type VaultDatum { owner: ByteArray, limits: Limits }. Read via InlineDatum and verify the transaction amount is within limits.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `state_machine` with StateDatum (state: Int, owner: ByteArray) using InlineDatum. Read the current state from own input and verify the continuing output has state + 1 in its InlineDatum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `loan_repay` with LoanDatum (borrower: ByteArray, principal: Int, interest: Int, due_date: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow repayment if borrower signed and amount >= principal + interest.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `treasury_spend` with ProposalDatum (recipient: ByteArray, amount: Int, votes_for: Int, votes_against: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend if votes_for > votes_against.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `collateral_vault` with CollateralDatum (borrower: ByteArray, loan_ref: ByteArray, liquidation_ratio: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow liquidation only if borrower signed or loan is in default.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `timed_release` with TimelockDatum (recipient: ByteArray, unlock_time: Int) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Release only after unlock_time using validity_range check.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `safe_escrow` with EscrowDatum via InlineDatum. Handle all three datum constructors: InlineDatum (proceed), DatumHash (reject with fail), NoDatum (reject with fail). Only process when datum is inline.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `acl_gate` with AccessDatum (allowed: List<ByteArray>, admin: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow transaction if any signer is in the allowed list or is the admin.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `dao_execute` with ProposalDatum (proposal_id: ByteArray, executor: ByteArray, quorum_met: Bool) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if quorum_met is True and executor signed.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `whitelist_sale` with WhitelistDatum (whitelist: List<ByteArray>, price: Int, max_per_wallet: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow purchase only if buyer is in whitelist.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `insurance_claim` with PolicyDatum (insured: ByteArray, coverage: Int, event_id: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow claim if insured signed and event matches datum event_id.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nft_sale` with ListingDatum (seller: ByteArray, price: Int, policy_id: ByteArray, asset_name: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum. Verify the seller receives at least price lovelace.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `reward_split` with SplitDatum (party_a: ByteArray, party_b: ByteArray, share_a_bps: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify outputs to party_a and party_b match their respective shares.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `optional_fee` with TxDatum (recipient: ByteArray, fee_address: Option<ByteArray>, amount: Int) via InlineDatum. If fee_address is Some, verify fee output exists; if None, proceed without fee.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_sale` with SaleDatum (seller: ByteArray, token_policy: ByteArray, token_name: ByteArray, price: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify seller gets price lovelace and buyer gets exactly 1 token.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `crowdfund` with FundDatum (goal: Int, deadline: Int, creator: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal by creator only if goal is met after deadline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `batch_transfer` with BatchDatum (recipients: List<ByteArray>, amounts: List<Int>, sender: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Verify sender signed and at least one output matches each recipient/amount pair.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `recurring_payment` with RecurringDatum (payer: ByteArray, payee: ByteArray, amount: Int, period_secs: Int, last_paid: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow payment if period_secs has elapsed since last_paid. Use interval.is_entirely_after(self.validity_range, last_paid + period_secs).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `parametric_vault` taking a policy_id: ByteArray parameter, with VaultDatum (owner: ByteArray, asset_name: ByteArray, min_tokens: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify owner signed and that at least one output contains >= min_tokens of (policy_id, asset_name) using assets.quantity_of. Use list.any(self.outputs, fn(o) { assets.quantity_of(o.value, policy_id, d.asset_name) >= d.min_tokens }). No helper functions needed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_release` that reads an EscrowDatum (with fields beneficiary: ByteArray and amount: Int) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify InlineDatum(raw) pattern, deserialize to EscrowDatum, and check that the beneficiary signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_timeout` with EscrowDatum containing beneficiary: ByteArray, deadline: Int, and refund_to: ByteArray. Use InlineDatum to read the datum from the continuing output. Allow release if beneficiary signed and deadline not passed, or refund if deadline passed and owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `oracle_consumer` that reads an OracleDatum (price: Int, timestamp: Int, source: ByteArray) from a reference input using InlineDatum. Verify the price is above a minimum threshold stored in the spend datum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_validator` with a SpendDatum (min_price: Int, owner: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains an OracleDatum with price >= min_price and that the owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_cliff` with VestingDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, cliff_time: Int, total_amount: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal only after cliff_time using interval.is_entirely_after.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_linear` with VestingDatum (owner: ByteArray, start_time: Int, end_time: Int, amount: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the owner signed and the transaction validity range is within vesting period.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_bid` with AuctionDatum (highest_bid: Int, bidder: ByteArray, deadline: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds current highest_bid and deadline hasn't passed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_close` that reads AuctionDatum (winner: ByteArray, amount: Int, seller: ByteArray) from InlineDatum. Allow close only after deadline and verify winner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `multisig_wallet` with a MultiSigDatum (required_signers: List<ByteArray>, threshold: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least threshold of the required_signers appear in extra_signatories.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `protocol_config` with ConfigDatum (admin: ByteArray, fee_bps: Int, paused: Bool) using InlineDatum. Allow actions only when not paused and admin signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `state_machine` with StateDatum (state: Int, owner: ByteArray) using InlineDatum. Read the current state from own input and verify the continuing output has state + 1 in its InlineDatum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nft_sale` with ListingDatum (seller: ByteArray, price: Int, policy_id: ByteArray, asset_name: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum. Verify the seller receives at least price lovelace.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `loan_repay` with LoanDatum (borrower: ByteArray, principal: Int, interest: Int, due_date: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow repayment if borrower signed and amount >= principal + interest.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `treasury_spend` with ProposalDatum (recipient: ByteArray, amount: Int, votes_for: Int, votes_against: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend if votes_for > votes_against.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `reward_split` with SplitDatum (party_a: ByteArray, party_b: ByteArray, share_a_bps: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify outputs to party_a and party_b match their respective shares.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `subscription` with SubDatum (subscriber: ByteArray, expiry: Int, fee_per_period: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow renewal if subscriber signed and fee is paid; allow cancel after expiry.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `collateral_vault` with CollateralDatum (borrower: ByteArray, loan_ref: ByteArray, liquidation_ratio: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow liquidation only if borrower signed or loan is in default.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `safe_escrow` with EscrowDatum via InlineDatum. Handle all three datum constructors: InlineDatum (proceed), DatumHash (reject with fail), NoDatum (reject with fail). Only process when datum is inline.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `acl_gate` with AccessDatum (allowed: List<ByteArray>, admin: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow transaction if any signer is in the allowed list or is the admin.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `upgradeable` with VersionedDatum (version: Int, admin: ByteArray, params: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow upgrade (version bump) only by admin; allow use by anyone when active.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_sale` with SaleDatum (seller: ByteArray, token_policy: ByteArray, token_name: ByteArray, price: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify seller gets price lovelace and buyer gets exactly 1 token.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `crowdfund` with FundDatum (goal: Int, deadline: Int, creator: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal by creator only if goal is met after deadline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `dao_execute` with ProposalDatum (proposal_id: ByteArray, executor: ByteArray, quorum_met: Bool) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if quorum_met is True and executor signed.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `whitelist_sale` with WhitelistDatum (whitelist: List<ByteArray>, price: Int, max_per_wallet: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow purchase only if buyer is in whitelist.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `parametric_vault` taking a policy_id: ByteArray parameter, with VaultDatum (owner: ByteArray, asset_name: ByteArray, min_tokens: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify owner signed and that at least one output contains >= min_tokens of (policy_id, asset_name) using assets.quantity_of. Use list.any(self.outputs, fn(o) { assets.quantity_of(o.value, policy_id, d.asset_name) >= d.min_tokens }). No helper functions needed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `insurance_claim` with PolicyDatum (insured: ByteArray, coverage: Int, event_id: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow claim if insured signed and event matches datum event_id.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `policy_payout` with CoverageDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, payout_amount: Int, claim_code: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow payout if the beneficiary has signed the transaction, the redeemer's submitted_code matches the datum's claim_code, and the payout_amount is greater than zero.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `market_rate_guard` with a RateLimit datum (floor_rate: Int, authorized_key: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that at least one reference input contains a PriceFeedDatum with rate strictly greater than floor_rate, and that the authorized_key has signed the transaction. Additionally, ensure the transaction produces at least one output containing more than 2,000,000 lovelace.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `debt_settlement` with RepaymentDatum (creditor: ByteArray, lender: ByteArray, debt_amount: Int, penalty_fee: Int, expiry: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow settlement only if both the creditor has signed the transaction AND the transaction occurs before the expiry deadline, ensuring the total obligation (debt_amount + penalty_fee) is greater than zero.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `bid_finalize` that reads BidRecord (bidder: ByteArray, bid_amount: Int, beneficiary: ByteArray, closing_time: Int) from InlineDatum. Allow finalization only before the closing time and verify that both the bidder and the beneficiary have signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `market_rate_validator` with a ThresholdDatum (floor_rate: Int, max_rate: Int, beneficiary: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains a RateDatum with a rate that falls within the inclusive range [floor_rate, max_rate], and that the beneficiary has signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `payment_release` that reads a `PaymentDatum` (with fields `recipient: ByteArray` and `locked_lovelace: Int`) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify the InlineDatum pattern, deserialize to `PaymentDatum`, and check that both the recipient signed the transaction AND that the locked amount is greater than zero.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `committee_approval` with a CommitteeDatum (members: List<ByteArray>, min_approvals: Int, expiry: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least min_approvals of the committee members appear in extra_signatories AND that the transaction validity range is entirely before the expiry slot, ensuring approvals are collected before the committee mandate expires.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_gate` that reads a FeedDatum (rate: Int, updated_at: Int, feed_id: ByteArray) from a reference input using InlineDatum. The spend datum stores a maximum allowed rate (ceiling), a feed policy ID, and a feed asset name. The validator succeeds only when the rate found in the oracle feed reference input is strictly below the ceiling stored in the spend datum, effectively acting as a price ceiling enforcement gate.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_proposal_spend` with VoteTally datum (beneficiary: ByteArray, lovelace_amount: Int, yea_votes: Int, nay_votes: Int, abstain_votes: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend only if yea_votes strictly exceeds the sum of nay_votes and abstain_votes (i.e., an absolute majority among all recorded votes).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `parametric_policy_claim` with CoverageDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, payout_amount: Int, incident_code: ByteArray, min_coverage: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow a claim if the beneficiary signed the transaction, the redeemer's incident code matches the datum's incident_code, and the payout_amount meets or exceeds the min_coverage threshold.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `versioned_config` with `ConfigDatum` (revision: Int, curator: ByteArray, threshold: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow a revision bump only by the curator, and enforce that the new threshold must be strictly greater than the current threshold (not just any change); allow general use by anyone. The continuing output must carry the updated datum with the same curator, the incremented revision, and the new (larger) threshold.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `scheduled_transfer` with SubscriptionDatum (subscriber: ByteArray, recipient: ByteArray, payment_lovelace: Int, interval_secs: Int, next_due: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow a payment if the current time is entirely after `next_due`. On a successful Pay redeemer, verify that the payee receives at least `payment_lovelace` lovelace, and that a continuing output at the script address carries an updated datum where `next_due` advances by `interval_secs`. Allow the subscriber to cancel at any time by signing with their key.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_vote_spend` with MotionDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, lovelace_amount: Int, approvals: Int, rejections: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend only if approvals are strictly greater than twice the number of rejections, enforcing a supermajority requirement.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `medical_benefit_claim` with a `BenefitPolicy` datum (beneficiary: ByteArray, benefit_amount: Int, procedure_code: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow a claim if the beneficiary has signed the transaction, the claimed procedure code matches the datum's procedure_code, and the benefit_amount is greater than zero.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `parametric_coverage_claim` with CoverageDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, payout_amount: Int, incident_code: ByteArray, min_coverage: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow a claim if the beneficiary has signed the transaction, the redeemer's submitted_incident_code matches the datum's incident_code, and the payout_amount is greater than or equal to the datum's min_coverage threshold.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `time_locked_payment` with PaymentDatum containing recipient: ByteArray, expiry: Int, and depositor: ByteArray. Allow release if the recipient has signed the transaction and the transaction is entirely after the expiry (funds become claimable after the lock period), or allow reclaim if the depositor has signed and the transaction is entirely before the expiry (depositor can cancel early). Use a Release/Reclaim redeemer to distinguish the two paths.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `threshold_approval` with a CouncilDatum (members: List<ByteArray>, min_approvals: Int, max_members: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that the council does not exceed max_members in size, and that at least min_approvals of the members appear in extra_signatories.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `campaign_vault` with CampaignDatum (target_lovelace: Int, expiry: Int, beneficiary: ByteArray, min_contribution: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow the beneficiary to claim funds only if the total lovelace across all inputs meets or exceeds the target and the transaction is submitted after the expiry. Additionally, enforce that the beneficiary has signed the transaction. Provide a Reclaim action that allows contributors to reclaim funds if the campaign has expired but the target was not met.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `multi_payout` with PayoutDatum (payees: List<ByteArray>, minimums: List<Int>, authorizer: ByteArray, expiry: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the authorizer signed the transaction, the transaction is submitted before the expiry deadline, the payees and minimums lists are non-empty and of equal implied length, and at least one output matches each payee/minimum pair (output to that verification key credential with lovelace strictly greater than the minimum amount).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `trade_settlement` with a `SettlementDatum` (via InlineDatum) containing fields: `vendor: ByteArray`, `client: ByteArray`, `price: Int`, and `expiry: Int`. The validator enforces that funds can only be released when both the vendor and client have signed the transaction, and that at least one output pays at least `price` lovelace to any address. If only the client has signed, the transaction is rejected. If only the vendor has signed, the transaction is also rejected. Both signatures are strictly required, making this a stricter two-party agreement than the original escrow.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `amm_liquidity_exit` with SwapPoolState (token_x_reserve: Int, token_y_reserve: Int, total_shares: Int) using InlineDatum. The validator accepts a redeemer with shares_to_burn, minimum_x_out, and minimum_y_out fields. It verifies that the shares_to_burn is strictly less than the total_shares (preventing full pool drain), that LP tokens are burned in the mint field matching the shares_to_burn amount, that the proportional withdrawal amounts meet the minimum slippage thresholds, and that the continuing pool output reflects correctly updated reserves and share supply.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_marketplace` with `MarketDatum` (vendor: ByteArray, ask_lovelace: Int, nft_policy: ByteArray, nft_asset: ByteArray, min_royalty: Int) read via InlineDatum. Verify that the vendor receives at least `ask_lovelace` lovelace AND that any single output to the vendor contains at least `min_royalty` lovelace (enforcing a minimum per-payment royalty floor in addition to the total price check).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `payment_unlock` that reads a `PaymentLock` datum (with fields `recipient: ByteArray` and `locked_value: Int`) from the spend input using the standard datum parameter. Verify that the locked value is greater than zero and that the recipient has signed the transaction. Both conditions must hold for the spend to be valid.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_execute` with GovernanceDatum (motion_id: ByteArray, authorized_agent: ByteArray, threshold_reached: Bool, minimum_votes: Int, actual_votes: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if threshold_reached is True, actual_votes meets or exceeds minimum_votes, and the authorized_agent has signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `permission_check` with a `PermissionDatum` containing `whitelist: List<ByteArray>` and `superuser: ByteArray` via InlineDatum. Allow the transaction only if ALL signatories present on the transaction are either in the whitelist or are the superuser (i.e., no unauthorized signer may participate). Additionally, there must be at least one signatory on the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `secured_loan_vault` with LoanCollateral datum (lender: ByteArray, position_id: ByteArray, health_factor: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal only if the lender signed the transaction. Allow forced_closure only if the lender signed or the position is underwater (is_underwater flag is true), and additionally the health_factor must be below 150 and the position must be referenced in a continuing output.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `credit_settlement` with CreditAgreement datum (lender: ByteArray, creditor: ByteArray, face_value: Int, fee: Int, expiry: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow settlement if both the lender and creditor have signed the transaction, the repayment output contains at least face_value + fee lovelace, and the transaction occurs strictly after the expiry date (i.e., late settlement is permitted but early settlement is not).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nft_auction_escrow` with BidState (top_price: Int, winner: ByteArray, end_time: Int, min_increment: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds the current top price by at least the minimum increment, the auction end time hasn't passed, and the continuing output locks at least the new bid amount in lovelace while preserving the end time and minimum increment fields.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `fee_distribution` with a `DistributionDatum` (recipient_x: ByteArray, recipient_y: ByteArray, recipient_z: ByteArray, portion_x_bps: Int, portion_y_bps: Int) via InlineDatum. The validator splits funds among three parties where portion_z is derived as 10000 - portion_x_bps - portion_y_bps. Verify that outputs to each recipient meet or exceed their calculated share of the total distributed amount, and that the basis point portions are valid (each non-negative, sum does not exceed 10000).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `bounded_treasury` where the datum contains a nested type: `pub type Range { lower: Int, upper: Int }` inside `pub type TreasuryDatum { custodian: ByteArray, allowed_range: Range, required_signers: Int }`. The validator reads the datum via InlineDatum and verifies that: (1) the total lovelace across all transaction outputs falls within the allowed range (inclusive), and (2) the number of extra signatories meets or exceeds the `required_signers` threshold.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_threshold_gate` taking a policy_id: ByteArray parameter, with GateDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, token_label: ByteArray, required_amount: Int, max_outputs: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the beneficiary signed the transaction, that at least one output contains >= required_amount of (policy_id, token_label) using assets.quantity_of, and that the total number of outputs does not exceed max_outputs. Use list.any for the token check and list.count for the output count constraint.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `presale_gated` with PresaleDatum (approved_buyers: List<ByteArray>, token_price: Int, purchase_cap: Int, min_purchase: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow purchase only if the buyer is in the approved_buyers list, the quantity is at least min_purchase and at most purchase_cap, the buyer has signed the transaction, and sufficient lovelace payment is sent back to the script address. The datum must be preserved on the continuing output.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `presale_gate` with PresaleDatum (approved_buyers: List<ByteArray>, token_price: Int, purchase_limit: Int, sale_deadline: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow purchase only if the buyer is in the approved_buyers list, the transaction is signed by the buyer, the quantity is between 1 and purchase_limit inclusive, the total payment meets the required amount, and the transaction occurs entirely before the sale_deadline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `locked_payment` with a `PaymentDatum` type (fields: `payer`, `payee`, `lovelace`, `expiry`) accessed via InlineDatum. Handle all three datum constructors: InlineDatum (proceed with logic), DatumHash (reject with fail), NoDatum (reject with fail). Support three redeemer actions: `Release` (requires both payer and payee signatures), `Cancel` (requires only payer signature), and `Claim` (requires only payee signature). Only process when datum is inline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `permission_gate` with a `PermissionDatum` containing `authorized_users: List<ByteArray>` and `super_user: ByteArray` via InlineDatum. Allow the transaction only if the super_user has signed AND at least one authorized user has also signed (requiring both conditions to be true, rather than either/or).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `timed_payment` with PaymentDatum containing recipient: ByteArray, expiry: Int, and sender: ByteArray. Allow release if the recipient has signed the transaction and the transaction occurs entirely after a specified start time (expiry used as earliest release time), or allow reclaim if the expiry has passed and the original sender signed the transaction. This inverts the typical escrow pattern by requiring the validity range to be entirely after the expiry for release, and allowing the sender to reclaim funds once the window has closed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_guard` that reads a FeedDatum (rate: Int, updated_at: Int, feed_id: ByteArray) from a reference input using InlineDatum. The spend datum stores a maximum allowed rate (acting as a price ceiling), a feed policy ID and asset name used to locate the reference input, an authorized operator public key hash, and a deadline after which the validator rejects all transactions. The validator succeeds only when the feed's rate is at or below the maximum rate, the transaction is signed by the operator, and the transaction's validity range ends entirely before the deadline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `exclusive_drop` with SaleDatum (approved_buyers: List<ByteArray>, unit_price: Int, purchase_limit: Int, treasury: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow purchase only if the buyer is in the approved_buyers list, has signed the transaction, purchases at least 1 and no more than the purchase_limit, and the total payment goes to the treasury address (verified by checking that at least one output to the treasury carries the required lovelace amount equal to unit_price times quantity).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_spend` with MotionDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, lovelace_amount: Int, approvals: Int, rejections: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend only if approvals are strictly greater than twice the number of rejections, enforcing a supermajority requirement.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `policy_settlement` with ContractDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, payout_amount: Int, claim_reference: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow settlement if the beneficiary has signed the transaction and the claim reference in the redeemer matches the one stored in the datum, and additionally the payout_amount must be greater than zero.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_action` with GovernanceRecord (action_id: ByteArray, authorized_agent: ByteArray, threshold_reached: Bool, min_signatures: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if threshold_reached is True, the authorized_agent has signed the transaction, and the total number of extra signatories meets or exceeds min_signatures.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_motion` with MotionDatum (motion_ref: ByteArray, authorized_agent: ByteArray, threshold_reached: Bool, vote_count: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if threshold_reached is True, the authorized_agent signed the transaction, and vote_count is greater than 10.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `timed_auction` with BidState (top_amount: Int, top_bidder: ByteArray, end_time: Int, min_increment: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds the current top_amount by at least min_increment, the auction end_time has not yet passed, and that the continuing output's lovelace covers the new top_amount. The min_increment field must remain unchanged between the current and new datum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `community_raise` with CampaignDatum (target: Int, expiry: Int, beneficiary: ByteArray, min_contribution: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal by the beneficiary only if the total lovelace across all inputs meets or exceeds the target and the transaction occurs after the expiry. Allow refund by anyone if the campaign has expired but the target was not met, provided each contributor receives back at least the minimum contribution amount (enforced by checking that every output contains at least min_contribution lovelace).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `market_price_guard` with a ThresholdDatum (max_price: Int, authorized_buyer: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that at least one reference input contains a MarketDatum with price <= max_price (a ceiling check instead of a floor check), and that the authorized_buyer has signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_vault` with VoteTally datum (beneficiary: ByteArray, lovelace_amount: Int, approvals: Int, rejections: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend only if approvals are strictly greater than twice the number of rejections, enforcing a supermajority requirement.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `market_order_validator` with a TradeConfig datum (threshold_price: Int, trader: ByteArray, max_price: Int) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains a PriceFeed datum with a current_price that falls within the inclusive range [threshold_price, max_price], and that the trader signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `english_auction` with BidState (current_price: Int, winner: ByteArray, end_time: Int, min_increment: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds the current price by at least the minimum increment, the auction end time has not passed, and the declared winner has signed the transaction. Additionally, ensure the continuing output holds enough lovelace to cover the new bid price.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `loan_position_vault` with LoanDatum (lender: ByteArray, position_id: ByteArray, health_factor: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal only if the lender signed the transaction. Allow forced closure only if the lender signed or the position is unhealthy (health_factor strictly less than 100).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `cross_chain_unlock` with TransferDatum (value: Int, beneficiary: ByteArray, sequence: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Verify the sequence number hasn't been processed by checking a reference input's SequenceDatum (processed_sequences: List<ByteArray>), require the beneficiary to sign the transaction, and enforce that the transferred value meets a minimum threshold of 1000 lovelace.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_fund_release` with BudgetProposal datum (beneficiary: ByteArray, lovelace_amount: Int, approvals: Int, rejections: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow the spend only if approvals are strictly greater than twice the number of rejections, enforcing a supermajority requirement before funds can be released.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nft_marketplace` with ListingDatum (vendor: ByteArray, asset_policy: ByteArray, asset_name: ByteArray, asking_price: Int, min_lovelace: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the vendor receives at least `asking_price` lovelace and the buyer receives exactly the listed NFT. Additionally enforce that the payment output contains at least `min_lovelace` to satisfy minimum UTxO requirements, and that the vendor's address is a verification key (not a script).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `loan_collateral_lock` with a `LoanCollateralDatum` (debtor: ByteArray, position_id: ByteArray, margin_threshold: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow a `DebtorRepay` action only if the debtor has signed the transaction. Allow a `ForceClose` action only if the current collateral ratio (provided in the redeemer) falls strictly below the margin threshold AND the transaction is entirely after a provided deadline (Int in the redeemer), meaning both conditions must hold simultaneously for forced liquidation.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `medical_benefit` with CoverageDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, benefit_amount: Int, procedure_code: ByteArray, max_claims: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow a benefit payout if the beneficiary has signed the transaction, the redeemer's procedure code matches the datum procedure_code, and the benefit_amount is greater than zero.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `bid_settlement` that reads BidRecord (bidder: ByteArray, bid_lovelace: Int, auctioneer: ByteArray, close_time: Int) from InlineDatum. Allow settlement only after close_time and verify that both the bidder and auctioneer have signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `conditional_routing` with PaymentSpec (beneficiary: ByteArray, treasury: Option<ByteArray>, min_lovelace: Int) via InlineDatum. The validator ensures the beneficiary output contains at least min_lovelace, and if a treasury address is provided, verifies that the treasury output contains at least 10% of min_lovelace (integer division); if treasury is None, the payment proceeds without any treasury routing requirement.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `exchange_settings` with SettingsDatum (operator: ByteArray, swap_fee_bps: Int, max_swap_bps: Int, frozen: Bool) using InlineDatum. Allow swap fee updates only when the new fee is within range and less than or equal to max_swap_bps, allow max_swap_bps updates only when the new max is at least as large as the current swap_fee_bps, allow freeze/unfreeze actions, and allow closing the settings UTxO — all gated by the operator's signature and the contract not being frozen (except for Unfreeze and Close actions).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_unlock_schedule` with UnlockDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, cliff_time: Int, maturity_time: Int, total_tokens: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the beneficiary signed the transaction and the validity range is entirely after the cliff_time but entirely before the maturity_time, ensuring tokens can only be claimed during the active unlock window.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `cooperative_spend` with a CoopDatum (participants: List<ByteArray>, min_approvals: Int, deadline: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least min_approvals of the participants appear in extra_signatories AND that the transaction is submitted entirely before the deadline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `mortgage_settle` with MortgageDatum (mortgagor: ByteArray, loan_amount: Int, penalty_fee: Int, expiry: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow settlement if the mortgagor has signed the transaction, the total payment in any output meets or exceeds loan_amount + penalty_fee, and the transaction validity range falls entirely after the expiry date (i.e., settlement is only permitted after the mortgage term has expired).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_release_schedule` with ReleaseDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, cliff_time: Int, maturity_time: Int, total_tokens: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the beneficiary signed the transaction, the transaction validity range starts after the cliff time, and the transaction validity range ends before the maturity time. The validator enforces a cliff-based token release where no withdrawal is permitted before the cliff and full release occurs at maturity.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `subscription_service` with SubscriptionDatum (subscriber: ByteArray, provider: ByteArray, fee: Int, interval_secs: Int, next_due: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow renewal if interval_secs has elapsed since next_due (i.e., the transaction is entirely after next_due). The provider must receive at least the fee in lovelace in one of the outputs. A continuing output must be present at the script address with updated next_due set to next_due + interval_secs, and all other fields unchanged. The subscriber may cancel by signing the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `presale_access` with PresaleDatum (approved_buyers: List<ByteArray>, unit_price: Int, purchase_limit: Int, sale_deadline: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow purchase only if the buyer is in the approved_buyers list, the transaction is signed by the buyer, the quantity is between 1 and purchase_limit inclusive, the total payment in lovelace meets or exceeds unit_price multiplied by quantity, and the transaction occurs entirely before the sale_deadline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `governance_settings` with SettingsDatum (governor: ByteArray, tax_rate: Int, locked: Bool) using InlineDatum. Allow spending only when the contract is not locked, the governor has signed the transaction, and the tax_rate is within a valid range (greater than 0 and at most 10000 basis points).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `flight_delay_claim` with FlightPolicy datum (passenger: ByteArray, payout_amount: Int, flight_code: ByteArray, min_delay_minutes: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow claim if the passenger has signed the transaction, the claimed flight code matches the datum flight code, and there must be at least two signatories on the transaction (requiring a witness beyond just the passenger, such as an oracle or airline representative).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_gate_vault` taking a policy_id: ByteArray parameter, with GateDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, token_name: ByteArray, threshold: Int, max_outputs: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the beneficiary signed the transaction, that at least one output contains >= threshold of (policy_id, token_name) using assets.quantity_of, and that the total number of outputs does not exceed max_outputs. Use list.any(self.outputs, fn(o) { assets.quantity_of(o.value, policy_id, d.token_name) >= d.threshold }) and list.count to enforce the output cap.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `subscription_stream` with SubscriptionDatum (subscriber: ByteArray, provider: ByteArray, fee_lovelace: Int, interval_secs: Int, next_due: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow a payment withdrawal if the current time is entirely after next_due. The validator should verify that the provider receives at least fee_lovelace, that a continuing output at the same script address carries an updated SubscriptionDatum with next_due advanced by interval_secs, and that the subscriber can cancel at any time by signing the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_unlock` with UnlockDatum (recipient: ByteArray, unlock_after: Int, min_lovelace: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal only after unlock_after using interval.is_entirely_after, and ensure the transaction is signed by the recipient. Additionally, verify that at least one output sends a minimum of min_lovelace to an address whose payment credential matches the recipient, without requiring a specific datum on that output.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_release` that reads an EscrowDatum (with fields beneficiary: ByteArray and amount: Int) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify InlineDatum(raw) pattern, deserialize to EscrowDatum, and check that the beneficiary signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `oracle_consumer` that reads an OracleDatum (price: Int, timestamp: Int, source: ByteArray) from a reference input using InlineDatum. Verify the price is above a minimum threshold stored in the spend datum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_validator` with a SpendDatum (min_price: Int, owner: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains an OracleDatum with price >= min_price and that the owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_cliff` with VestingDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, cliff_time: Int, total_amount: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal only after cliff_time using interval.is_entirely_after.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_linear` with VestingDatum (owner: ByteArray, start_time: Int, end_time: Int, amount: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the owner signed and the transaction validity range is within vesting period.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_bid` with AuctionDatum (highest_bid: Int, bidder: ByteArray, deadline: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds current highest_bid and deadline hasn't passed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_close` that reads AuctionDatum (winner: ByteArray, amount: Int, seller: ByteArray) from InlineDatum. Allow close only after deadline and verify winner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `multisig_wallet` with a MultiSigDatum (required_signers: List<ByteArray>, threshold: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least threshold of the required_signers appear in extra_signatories.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `protocol_config` with ConfigDatum (admin: ByteArray, fee_bps: Int, paused: Bool) using InlineDatum. Allow actions only when not paused and admin signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nested_config` where the datum contains a nested type: pub type Limits { min: Int, max: Int } inside pub type VaultDatum { owner: ByteArray, limits: Limits }. Read via InlineDatum and verify the transaction amount is within limits.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `state_machine` with StateDatum (state: Int, owner: ByteArray) using InlineDatum. Read the current state from own input and verify the continuing output has state + 1 in its InlineDatum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `lp_withdraw` with PoolDatum (reserve_a: Int, reserve_b: Int, lp_supply: Int) using InlineDatum. Verify the LP token burn amount matches the proportional share of reserves.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `loan_repay` with LoanDatum (borrower: ByteArray, principal: Int, interest: Int, due_date: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow repayment if borrower signed and amount >= principal + interest.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `treasury_spend` with ProposalDatum (recipient: ByteArray, amount: Int, votes_for: Int, votes_against: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend if votes_for > votes_against.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `reward_split` with SplitDatum (party_a: ByteArray, party_b: ByteArray, share_a_bps: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify outputs to party_a and party_b match their respective shares.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `collateral_vault` with CollateralDatum (borrower: ByteArray, loan_ref: ByteArray, liquidation_ratio: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow liquidation only if borrower signed or loan is in default.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `acl_gate` with AccessDatum (allowed: List<ByteArray>, admin: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow transaction if any signer is in the allowed list or is the admin.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `upgradeable` with VersionedDatum (version: Int, admin: ByteArray, params: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow upgrade (version bump) only by admin; allow use by anyone when active.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_sale` with SaleDatum (seller: ByteArray, token_policy: ByteArray, token_name: ByteArray, price: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify seller gets price lovelace and buyer gets exactly 1 token.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `crowdfund` with FundDatum (goal: Int, deadline: Int, creator: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal by creator only if goal is met after deadline.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `dao_execute` with ProposalDatum (proposal_id: ByteArray, executor: ByteArray, quorum_met: Bool) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if quorum_met is True and executor signed.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `whitelist_sale` with WhitelistDatum (whitelist: List<ByteArray>, price: Int, max_per_wallet: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow purchase only if buyer is in whitelist.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `parametric_vault` taking a policy_id: ByteArray parameter, with VaultDatum (owner: ByteArray, asset_name: ByteArray, min_tokens: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify owner signed and that at least one output contains >= min_tokens of (policy_id, asset_name) using assets.quantity_of. Use list.any(self.outputs, fn(o) { assets.quantity_of(o.value, policy_id, d.asset_name) >= d.min_tokens }). No helper functions needed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `insurance_claim` with PolicyDatum (insured: ByteArray, coverage: Int, event_id: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow claim if insured signed and event matches datum event_id.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `batch_transfer` with BatchDatum (recipients: List<ByteArray>, amounts: List<Int>, sender: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Verify sender signed and at least one output matches each recipient/amount pair.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_release` that reads an EscrowDatum (with fields beneficiary: ByteArray and amount: Int) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify InlineDatum(raw) pattern, deserialize to EscrowDatum, and check that the beneficiary signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_timeout` with EscrowDatum containing beneficiary: ByteArray, deadline: Int, and refund_to: ByteArray. Use InlineDatum to read the datum from the continuing output. Allow release if beneficiary signed and deadline not passed, or refund if deadline passed and owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_release` that reads an EscrowDatum (with fields beneficiary: ByteArray and amount: Int) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify InlineDatum(raw) pattern, deserialize to EscrowDatum, and check that the beneficiary signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_timeout` with EscrowDatum containing beneficiary: ByteArray, deadline: Int, and refund_to: ByteArray. Use InlineDatum to read the datum from the continuing output. Allow release if beneficiary signed and deadline not passed, or refund if deadline passed and owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `oracle_consumer` that reads an OracleDatum (price: Int, timestamp: Int, source: ByteArray) from a reference input using InlineDatum. Verify the price is above a minimum threshold stored in the spend datum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_validator` with a SpendDatum (min_price: Int, owner: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains an OracleDatum with price >= min_price and that the owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_cliff` with VestingDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, cliff_time: Int, total_amount: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal only after cliff_time using interval.is_entirely_after.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_linear` with VestingDatum (owner: ByteArray, start_time: Int, end_time: Int, amount: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify the owner signed and the transaction validity range is within vesting period.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_bid` with AuctionDatum (highest_bid: Int, bidder: ByteArray, deadline: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds current highest_bid and deadline hasn't passed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_close` that reads AuctionDatum (winner: ByteArray, amount: Int, seller: ByteArray) from InlineDatum. Allow close only after deadline and verify winner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `multisig_wallet` with a MultiSigDatum (required_signers: List<ByteArray>, threshold: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least threshold of the required_signers appear in extra_signatories.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `protocol_config` with ConfigDatum (admin: ByteArray, fee_bps: Int, paused: Bool) using InlineDatum. Allow actions only when not paused and admin signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nested_config` where the datum contains a nested type: pub type Limits { min: Int, max: Int } inside pub type VaultDatum { owner: ByteArray, limits: Limits }. Read via InlineDatum and verify the transaction amount is within limits.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `state_machine` with StateDatum (state: Int, owner: ByteArray) using InlineDatum. Read the current state from own input and verify the continuing output has state + 1 in its InlineDatum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nft_sale` with ListingDatum (seller: ByteArray, price: Int, policy_id: ByteArray, asset_name: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum. Verify the seller receives at least price lovelace.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `loan_repay` with LoanDatum (borrower: ByteArray, principal: Int, interest: Int, due_date: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow repayment if borrower signed and amount >= principal + interest.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `treasury_spend` with ProposalDatum (recipient: ByteArray, amount: Int, votes_for: Int, votes_against: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow spend if votes_for > votes_against.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `reward_split` with SplitDatum (party_a: ByteArray, party_b: ByteArray, share_a_bps: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify outputs to party_a and party_b match their respective shares.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `collateral_vault` with CollateralDatum (borrower: ByteArray, loan_ref: ByteArray, liquidation_ratio: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow liquidation only if borrower signed or loan is in default.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `bridge_release` with BridgeDatum (amount: Int, recipient: ByteArray, nonce: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Verify the nonce hasn't been used by checking a reference input's NonceDatum.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `acl_gate` with AccessDatum (allowed: List<ByteArray>, admin: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow transaction if any signer is in the allowed list or is the admin.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `upgradeable` with VersionedDatum (version: Int, admin: ByteArray, params: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow upgrade (version bump) only by admin; allow use by anyone when active.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `optional_fee` with TxDatum (recipient: ByteArray, fee_address: Option<ByteArray>, amount: Int) via InlineDatum. If fee_address is Some, verify fee output exists; if None, proceed without fee.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `token_sale` with SaleDatum (seller: ByteArray, token_policy: ByteArray, token_name: ByteArray, price: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify seller gets price lovelace and buyer gets exactly 1 token.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `dao_execute` with ProposalDatum (proposal_id: ByteArray, executor: ByteArray, quorum_met: Bool) via InlineDatum. Allow execution only if quorum_met is True and executor signed.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `whitelist_sale` with WhitelistDatum (whitelist: List<ByteArray>, price: Int, max_per_wallet: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow purchase only if buyer is in whitelist.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `parametric_vault` taking a policy_id: ByteArray parameter, with VaultDatum (owner: ByteArray, asset_name: ByteArray, min_tokens: Int) via InlineDatum. Verify owner signed and that at least one output contains >= min_tokens of (policy_id, asset_name) using assets.quantity_of. Use list.any(self.outputs, fn(o) { assets.quantity_of(o.value, policy_id, d.asset_name) >= d.min_tokens }). No helper functions needed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `insurance_claim` with PolicyDatum (insured: ByteArray, coverage: Int, event_id: ByteArray) via InlineDatum. Allow claim if insured signed and event matches datum event_id.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `recurring_payment` with RecurringDatum (payer: ByteArray, payee: ByteArray, amount: Int, period_secs: Int, last_paid: Int) via InlineDatum. Allow payment if period_secs has elapsed since last_paid. Use interval.is_entirely_after(self.validity_range, last_paid + period_secs).

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `escrow_release` that reads an EscrowDatum (with fields beneficiary: ByteArray and amount: Int) from the transaction output using InlineDatum. Verify InlineDatum(raw) pattern, deserialize to EscrowDatum, and check that the beneficiary signed the transaction.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `oracle_consumer` that reads an OracleDatum (price: Int, timestamp: Int, source: ByteArray) from a reference input using InlineDatum. Verify the price is above a minimum threshold stored in the spend datum.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `price_feed_validator` with a SpendDatum (min_price: Int, owner: ByteArray) read via InlineDatum from the own input. Check that a reference input contains an OracleDatum with price >= min_price and that the owner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `vesting_cliff` with VestingDatum (beneficiary: ByteArray, cliff_time: Int, total_amount: Int) using InlineDatum. Allow withdrawal only after cliff_time using interval.is_entirely_after.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_bid` with AuctionDatum (highest_bid: Int, bidder: ByteArray, deadline: Int) using InlineDatum on the continuing output. Verify the new bid exceeds current highest_bid and deadline hasn't passed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `auction_close` that reads AuctionDatum (winner: ByteArray, amount: Int, seller: ByteArray) from InlineDatum. Allow close only after deadline and verify winner signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `multisig_wallet` with a MultiSigDatum (required_signers: List<ByteArray>, threshold: Int) read via InlineDatum. Check that at least threshold of the required_signers appear in extra_signatories.
Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `protocol_config` with ConfigDatum (admin: ByteArray, fee_bps: Int, paused: Bool) using InlineDatum. Allow actions only when not paused and admin signed.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `nested_config` where the datum contains a nested type: pub type Limits { min: Int, max: Int } inside pub type VaultDatum { owner: ByteArray, limits: Limits }. Read via InlineDatum and verify the transaction amount is within limits.

Write an Aiken v3 spend validator `state_machine` with StateDatum (state: Int, owner: ByteArray) using InlineDatum. Read the current state from own input and verify the continuing output has state + 1 in its InlineDatum.
